title: Dan Towriss: The ‘Passion & Drive’ Behind Cadillac F1 Team | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The newest team in Formula 1 are here to compete. Cadillac have earned the respect of their rivals. Now they’re pushing to get faster.

CEO Dan Towriss takes Tom Clarkson back to the start of the story - how he and his TWG Motorsports company first became involved in discussions about F1, conversations with General Motors and Cadillac, the work to build a team, choosing their race drivers and making their F1 debut in Australia.

Sergio Perez and Valtteri Bottas are the first Cadillac F1 drivers.

Showing all the original style qualities of  the driver who burst into F1 in 2023, Oscar looked right at home in the new McLaren MCL40-Mercedes, proving not only to be quick through the demanding T9-T10 section but also, simultaneously, over the lap as a whole. title: New F1 fans get LOST watching races... this guide explains EVERYTHING in 60 seconds
description:
New to F1? This will save you on race day 🏁

If you’re a new Formula One fan, race broadcasts can look overwhelming. So many numbers. So many abbreviations. So much happening at once.

Relax. It’s way simpler than it looks.

At the very top, you’ll see the total number of laps and how many have been completed. That tells you how far into the race you are.

On the left side of the screen is the leaderboard. Each driver’s current position is shown next to their name. If someone overtakes, you’ll

title: F1 TESTING EXPLAINED: Why the Fastest Lap is a LIE! 🤫🏎️
description:
Don't be fooled by the Bahrain timing screens! 🏁 In this video, we dive deep into how F1 Winter Testing actually works. From fuel loads to engine mapping and "sandbagging," I’ll explain why the record times you see don’t tell the full story for the 2026 season.

We analyze:

The "Sandbagging" strategy 🎒
Fuel levels & weight impact ⛽
Why teams hide their true pace 🔴
Subscribe for more technical F1 analysis!

#F1 #F1Testing #Formula1 #BahrainTest #F12026 #TechnicalAnalysis #Motorsport

title: 2026 F1 Engine & Battery Deployement Explained (MGU-K)
description:
F1 2026 just introduced one tiny word in the regulations — “pending” — and it completely changes how drivers use the throttle on corner exit.

In this video I break down what a power-limited pending period actually means in driver language, why the car no longer gives a sudden hybrid “kick”, and how the 150kW rule forces drivers to commit to throttle earlier instead of waiting for a boost moment.

You’ll learn:
• What “power-limited” vs “traction-limited” really feels like in the car
• Why the b
